name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')
Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
Improper Neutralization of Argument Delimiters in a Command ('Argument Injection')
Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')
Improper Control of Resource Identifiers ('Resource Injection')
Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer
Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')
Improper Handling of Length Parameter Inconsistency
evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:145 - Photo constructor receives file.getSize() as fileSize but actual photoData byte array stored separately; no validation that byte array length matches declared fileSize parameter.

name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
Missing Initialization of a Variable
evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:113-115 - Variables 'width', 'height', and 'photoData' initialized to null; conditionally assigned within try block. If exception occurs before assignment, variables remain null and are used downstream without null checks.

name: Use of Uninitialized Variable
evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:83 - file.getContentType() can return null per MultipartFile API; calling .toLowerCase() on potential null value will throw NullPointerException. Also lines 125-126: width/height passed to setWidth/setHeight without null validation when image is null.

name: Use of Singleton Pattern Without Synchronization in a Multithreaded Context
Unsynchronized Access to Shared Data in a Multithreaded Context
Unchecked Input for Loop Condition
evidence: HomeController.java:57-68 - Loop iterates over 'files' List<MultipartFile> from @RequestParam without bounds checking maximum file count; no limit on number of files an attacker can upload in a single request.

name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')
Improper Neutralization of Data within XQuery Expressions ('XQuery Injection')
Operation on a Resource after Expiration or Release
Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
Missing Release of Resource after Effective Lifetime
Missing Release of File Descriptor or Handle after Effective Lifetime
Access of Memory Location Before Start of Buffer
Access of Memory Location After End of Buffer
Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value
evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:119 - file.getBytes() reads entire file into memory without per-request memory limit enforcement; multiple concurrent large file uploads could exhaust JVM heap memory (DoS risk).

name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
evidence: application.properties:11-12 - spring.datasource.username=photoalbum, spring.datasource.password=photoalbum; application-docker.properties:3-4 - same hardcoded credentials

name: Buffer Access with Incorrect Length Value
Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition ('Infinite Loop')
Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component
evidence: PhotoFileController.java:38-62 - Controller directly accesses photo binary data (photoData, originalFileName, mimeType) from Photo entity instead of delegating to a dedicated service method; data access logic should be abstracted in the service layer.
